Ntc And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Sep-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:


Appearance:
Gregory H. McCormick, Esq., for Jun Zhong, Defendant/Moving Party
Steven J. Bell, Esq., for Plaintiff Jun Zhong, Plaintiff/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Company's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 2-4, 5-8, 9-12, 17-20, 21-24, 25-28, 41-44, 48-50, 51-53, 54-56, 57-59, 60-62, 63-65, 66-68, 69-71, 7274, 75-77, 78-80, 81-83, 84-86, 87-89, 90-92, 93-95, 9698, 99-101, 102-104, 105-106, 108-109, 111-113, 114-116, 117-119, and 131-133.
The motion is granted as to Request For Production Of Document No. 2 as Plaintiff is ordered to produce all photographs within 15 days of notice of this order.
The motion is denied as to Request For Production Of Document Nos. 4 and 5 as the requests seek qualified work product and there is no showing of good cause.
The motion is denied as to Request For Production Of Document Nos. 7, 8, and 9 as they are overly broad.
The motion is granted as to Request For Production Of Document Nos. 10 and 28.
Further responses shall be made and documents shall be produced within 15 days of notice of this order.
The responses and supplemental responses to the Special Interrogatories were inadequate and a misuse of the discovery process. The opposition as to the Special Interrogatories was made without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $925, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
